name: Smoked Salmon Spirals
recipeIngredient: 8 10-inch flour tortillas (or up to 12 inches in diameter)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the cream cheese, lemon juice, and rind in a bowl. Add scallions, dill, and paprika. Mix well.
2. Stir in the capers.
3. Spread each tortilla with about one-eighth of the cream-cheese mixture, leaving a quarter-inch margin around the edge.
4. Place slices of smoked salmon over the cream cheese on each tortilla.
5. Roll up each tortilla tightly, pressing down firmly as you roll to ensure it's compact.
6. Wrap each rolled tortilla in plastic wrap.
7. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours but no more than 12 hours.
8. To serve, slice the rolls into half-inch thick pieces.
9. Use the uneven ends for personal consumption if desired.
10. Arrange the slices on a serving dish and garnish each with a small dill sprig.
Makes about 60 spirals (including the uneven ends).
